sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Dime Community Bancshares, Inc. Reports Strong Fourth Quarter Results with Earnings Per Share Increasing By 15% On a Linked Quarter Basis
feed.summary: Dime Community Bancshares, Inc. reported strong fourth-quarter 2025 results, with diluted EPS of $0.68 and adjusted diluted EPS (non-GAAP) of $0.79, an 88% year-over-year increase. The company achieved record quarterly revenue of $124 million, driven by significant growth in core deposits and business loans. Strategic execution led to improved return metrics, efficiency levels, and solid asset quality, alongside a reduction in the CRE concentration ratio.

feed.title: Ulta Beauty stock hits all-time high at 675.95 USD By Investing.com
feed.summary: Ulta Beauty Inc. has reached an all-time high stock price of $675.95, reflecting a 63.82% increase over the past year. This performance highlights the company's robust growth and investor confidence, with a market capitalization nearing $30 billion. However, InvestingPro analysis suggests the stock might be overvalued, with technical indicators showing it's in overbought territory.

feed.title: Halliburton Prepares to Quickly Reenter Venezuelan Market
feed.summary: Halliburton's CEO, Jeff Miller, expressed confidence that the company could quickly re-establish its business in Venezuela. Analysts estimate that rehabilitating Venezuela's oil fields could require $10 billion annually, with a significant portion going to oilfield-service companies like Halliburton. Miller noted strong interest in Halliburton's presence in the country.

feed.title: OXY Receives 'Equal-Weight' Rating from Barclays Analyst | OXY S
feed.summary: Barclays analyst Betty Jiang has reinstated an 'Equal-Weight' rating on Occidental Petroleum (OXY). This latest rating follows a period where several other analysts lowered their price targets for OXY. Despite the cautious analyst sentiment, the average one-year price target from 23 analysts suggests an upside potential of 13.30% from the current price, while GuruFocus's GF Value estimates a downside.

feed.title: Nvidia, Broadcom, Marvell Poised To Benefit From AI-Driven Semiconductor Upswing, JPMorgan Says
feed.summary: JPMorgan analyst Harlan Sur expects semiconductor companies to perform well in Q4 2025 and into 2026, driven by AI demand and improving cyclical trends. He anticipates strong earnings and positive revisions, with AI infrastructure spending and tight supply chains contributing to a supportive environment for key suppliers. Sur highlighted Nvidia, Broadcom, and Marvell Technology, among others, as top picks due to their leverage in AI and accelerated compute spending.

feed.title: Earnings call transcript: Teledyne Technologies Q4 2025 beats forecasts, stock surges
feed.summary: Teledyne Technologies (TDY) reported strong Q4 2025 results, exceeding EPS and revenue forecasts, leading to a 4.2% pre-market stock surge. The company posted an EPS of $6.30 against a forecast of $5.84 and revenue of $1.61 billion, surpassing the $1.57 billion expectation. Teledyne also provided an optimistic outlook for 2026, projecting $6.37 billion in revenue and $23.65 non-GAAP EPS, driven by its diverse portfolio, particularly in digital imaging and unmanned markets.

feed.title: Resman Energy Technology Sold to SLB
feed.summary: SLB (NYSE: SLB) has acquired RESMAN Energy Technology from Nordic Capital, a move advised by Pickering Energy Partners. RESMAN specializes in wireless reservoir surveillance and tracer solutions, crucial for optimizing production and recovery in oil and gas, CO2 storage, and geothermal applications. This acquisition positions RESMAN for further growth and accelerates the adoption of its technology across various energy sectors.

feed.title: Northern Trust Q4 2025 earnings preview
feed.summary: This is an earnings preview for Northern Trust (NTRS) for the fourth quarter of 2025. Investors are anticipating the release of their financial results. The article indicates that more detailed information will follow.

feed.title: VeriSign Inc (VRSN) Shares Up 2.81% on Jan 21
feed.summary: VeriSign Inc (VRSN) shares jumped 2.81% on January 21, reaching an intraday high of $255.63 and closing at $255.08. This places the stock significantly above its 52-week low but still below its 52-week high. Wall Street analysts forecast an average target price of $299.51, indicating an "Outperform" status, while GuruFocus estimates a fair value of $275.25.

feed.title: Las Vegas Sands Stock Is Going Off – But Is It Actually Worth Your Money?
feed.summary: This article analyzes Las Vegas Sands (LVS) stock, questioning if the current hype makes it a worthwhile investment or a high-risk gamble. It highlights the company's strong ties to Asian tourism markets, its brand visibility through social media, and the significant risks associated with regulation and economic slowdowns. The author concludes with a "Cop or Drop" verdict, advising that LVS is a calculated gamble suitable for investors comfortable with volatility and long-term travel and casino cycles.

feed.title: These Analysts Increase Their Forecasts On Fifth Third Bancorp After Upbeat Q4 Earnings
feed.summary: Fifth Third Bancorp (NASDAQ: FITB) reported better-than-expected Q4 earnings of $1.08 per share, beating consensus estimates, despite missing sales estimates. Following these results, RBC Capital and Keefe, Bruyette & Woods analysts increased their price targets for the company. The bank's shares saw a positive reaction, gaining 3.1%.

feed.title: Newmont Stock Gained 4% Last Week. Can the Stock Rally Past $150?
feed.summary: Newmont Corporation's stock gained over 4% last week amidst geopolitical tensions driving a "Fear Trade" into gold, with bullion prices surging. TIKR's Advanced Valuation Model suggests a price target of $153, indicating a potential upside of 28.8% due to the company's strong free cash flow generation and operational milestones like the Ahafo North project. The article highlights Newmont as a "Double Hedge" offering protection against market volatility and growth from its internal projects.

feed.title: The Great Re-Entry: How Blackstone and KKR are Leading a Private Equity Renaissance in 2026
feed.summary: In 2026, the private equity sector is experiencing a "dealmaking renaissance" led by Blackstone (BX) and KKR & Co. Inc. (KKR), driven by stabilized interest rates and a strategic focus on AI infrastructure. Both firms are aggressively deploying capital into "real assets" like data centers and power generation, becoming key financiers of the technological and energy transition. This shift marks the end of a capital preservation era and the beginning of high-velocity deployment, transforming the US asset management landscape.

feed.title: CTVA: JP Morgan Raises Price Target to $75 While Maintaining Overweight Rating | CTVA Stock News
feed.summary: JP Morgan has increased its price target for Corteva (CTVA) from $70.00 to $75.00, while reiterating an "Overweight" rating, signifying continued confidence in the stock. This adjustment comes amidst other recent analyst rating changes, with the average one-year price target from 21 analysts for CTVA now being $78.12, suggesting a 10.30% upside from its current price. Corteva, an agricultural inputs company focusing on seeds and crop protection, plans to spin off its seeds business in 2026 to become a pure-play crop protection company.

feed.title: TE Connectivity (TEL) Reports Strong Q1 2026 Earnings and Growth Outlook
feed.summary: TE Connectivity (TEL) reported record Q1 2026 earnings with orders exceeding $5 billion, a 20% sales growth, and adjusted earnings per share of $2.72. The company, a major electrical connector supplier, demonstrated strong financial health with robust revenue growth and profitability. However, the report also highlighted potential overvaluation and insider selling activity, suggesting caution for investors despite strong institutional interest.

feed.title: Southern Company Tops FORTUNE's 2026 Most Admired Companies List
feed.summary: Southern Company has been named the top company in the electric and gas utilities sector for 2026 by FORTUNE, recognized for its financial strength and long-term investment value. CEO Chris Womack attributed this success to the dedication of its 28,000 employees. The company's consistent ranking since 1997 underscores its innovation, social responsibility, and extensive service network across the Southeast, serving 9 million customers.

feed.title: ADM Analyst Update: JP Morgan Raises Price Target while Maintaining Underweight Rating | ADM Stock News
feed.summary: JP Morgan analyst Thomas Palmer has raised the price target for Archer-Daniels-Midland (ADM) to $60 from $59, while maintaining an "Underweight" rating. This adjustment reflects ongoing market evaluations of ADM's performance and potential, as other analysts have also recently updated their ratings and price targets for the agricultural commodities giant. ADM is a major processor of agricultural commodities and also has a significant nutrition business.

feed.title: Hershey’s Goes for the Gold with Olympic Medal Chocolates
feed.summary: Hershey's is launching limited-edition milk chocolate medals, inspired by the Olympic and Paralympic Winter Games, as part of its first major marketing campaign since 2018. These gold-foil wrapped chocolates will be available online starting February 13th, with early access events in Pennsylvania and Times Square. The campaign, "Hershey’s. It’s Your Happy Place.", features five Team U.S.A. athletes sharing their personal stories.

feed.title: Applied Materials stock rises as AMAT steadies with markets; Needham lifts target to $390
feed.summary: Applied Materials (AMAT) shares rose 1.7% to $323.62 following a broader market rebound, despite previous declines. Needham reiterated a "Strong Buy" rating and increased its price target from $260 to $390, citing improved broad-based demand. Investors are keenly awaiting Q4 earnings reports from the chip sector, including Applied Materials' report on February 12th, for insights into capital spending and potential impacts from U.S. export restrictions.

feed.title: Dominion Energy preps for possible winter storm
feed.summary: Dominion Energy is preparing for a potential winter storm in Hampton Roads by bolstering its power grid to improve outage response. The company has hardened its infrastructure with stronger poles and smart technology to detect outages efficiently, especially concerning ice accumulation. Dominion Energy advises customers not to touch downed power lines and to take preventative measures at home, such as sealing drafts and being mindful of space heater use.

feed.title: 40 teen scientists vie for $1.8M at Regeneron talent search
feed.summary: Regeneron (NASDAQ: REGN) and Society for Science have announced the top 40 finalists for the 2026 Regeneron Science Talent Search, who will compete for over $1.8 million in awards, including a $250,000 top prize. These finalists, representing 35 schools across 15 states, were chosen from more than 2,600 entrants based on their original research. The competition, which is the nation's oldest and most prestigious science and math competition for high school seniors, will culminate in a public exhibition and an awards ceremony in Washington, D.C.

feed.title: Corning (GLW) Earnings Expected to Grow: Should You Buy?
feed.summary: Corning (GLW) is projected to report increased earnings and higher revenues for the quarter ended December 2025, with expectations of $0.70 EPS and $4.35 billion in revenue. The company has a Zacks Earnings ESP of +1.72% and a Zacks Rank of #2, indicating a high likelihood of an earnings beat when results are released on January 28. This positive outlook is further supported by Corning's consistent history of beating EPS estimates over the past four quarters.

feed.title: Upwork Inc (UPWK) Shares Gap Down to $19.21 on Jan 20
feed.summary: Upwork Inc (UPWK) shares gapped down on January 20th, trading at $19.21 after falling 3.32%. The stock is 13.93% below its 52-week high but 72.60% above its 52-week low. Analyst forecasts suggest an average target price of $23.60, implying a 22.85% upside, with an "Outperform" recommendation from brokerage firms, although GuruFocus estimates a GF Value of $15.14, suggesting a potential downside.

feed.title: Honda Shuts Down Joint Fuel-Cell Production with GM
feed.summary: Honda is ending its joint fuel-cell production venture with General Motors by the end of 2026, marking a shift towards independently developed hydrogen systems. This move aligns with Honda's long-term strategy for carbon neutrality by 2050 and emphasizes hydrogen as a core energy carrier. The Brownstown, Michigan facility, a joint venture since 2017, produced systems that powered vehicles like the CR-V e:FCEV.

feed.title: ROYAL CARIBBEAN GROUP NAMED TO THE FORTUNE WORLD'S MOST ADMIRED COMPANIES™ 2026 LIST FOR THE FIRST TIME
feed.summary: Royal Caribbean Group (NYSE: RCL) has been named to the Fortune World's Most Admired Companies 2026 list for the first time, recognizing its global reputation for innovation, leadership, and culture. The annual ranking, compiled by Fortune in partnership with Korn Ferry, surveys executives, directors, and analysts on nine key reputation attributes. This achievement reflects the company's strategic momentum, including the introduction of innovative ships, expansion of private destinations, and loyalty program enhancements across its brands.

feed.title: ROYAL CARIBBEAN GROUP NAMED TO THE FORTUNE WORLD'S MOST ADMIRED COMPANIES™ 2026 LIST FOR THE FIRST TIME
feed.summary: Royal Caribbean Group has been recognized for the first time on the Fortune World's Most Admired Companies 2026 list, acknowledging its global reputation for innovation, leadership, and culture. The company's Chairman and CEO, Jason Liberty, attributed this recognition to the dedication of their teams. This distinction follows a period of strategic growth and innovation, including new ship classes, expanded private destinations, and enhanced loyalty programs across its brands.

feed.title: Southwest Airlines (LUV) Expected to Beat Earnings Estimates: What to Know Ahead of Q4 Release
feed.summary: Southwest Airlines (LUV) is projected to surpass its Q4 2025 earnings estimates, with expected earnings of $0.55 per share and revenues of $7.49 billion, despite a year-over-year decline in EPS. This positive outlook is supported by a 14.06% upward revision in consensus EPS estimates over the last 30 days and a strong Zacks Earnings ESP of +7.27% combined with a Zacks Rank #2. The company has a history of beating EPS estimates, exceeding them in three of the last four quarters.

feed.title: Conocophillips (COP) Receives a Buy from Wells Fargo
feed.summary: Wells Fargo analyst Sam Margolin maintained a Buy rating on Conocophillips (COP) with a price target of $132.00, following a previous Buy rating from TipRanks Google Oil and Gas Exploration and Production. This stands in contrast to J.P. Morgan, which recently downgraded COP to a Hold, and BofA, which downgraded it to Underperform due to valuation concerns. The article also notes recent corporate insider stock sales and the company's latest quarterly financials.

feed.title: Analysts Have Conflicting Sentiments on These Real Estate Companies: Welltower (WELL) and Smith Douglas Homes Corp. Class A (SDHC)
feed.summary: This article examines analyst sentiments on two real estate companies, Welltower (WELL) and Smith Douglas Homes Corp. Class A (SDHC). Welltower received a "Strong Buy" consensus with a 10.6% upside, based on a Bank of America Securities analyst's Buy rating. In contrast, Smith Douglas Homes Corp. Class A has a "Hold" consensus, with Wells Fargo maintaining a Hold rating and an average price target indicating an 18.9% downside from current levels.

feed.title: United Airlines reports record revenue, raises 2026 EPS outlook
feed.summary: United Airlines Holdings Inc (NASDAQ:UAL, XETRA:UAL1) announced record fourth-quarter operating revenue of $15.4 billion and adjusted earnings per share of $3.10, exceeding analyst expectations. The increase was driven by rising premium and loyalty revenues. The airline also raised its adjusted EPS outlook for 2026 to $12 to $14, projecting continued growth despite challenges like government shutdowns and Boeing delivery delays.

feed.title: Wells Fargo Reaffirms Their Buy Rating on Delek US Holdings (DK)
feed.summary: Wells Fargo analyst Sam Margolin reaffirmed a Buy rating on Delek US Holdings (DK) on January 18, setting a price target of $53.00, while Mizuho Securities also issued a Buy rating. However, xAI reiterated a Hold rating on the stock. Delek US Holdings reported Q3 revenue of $2.89 billion and a net profit of $178 million, but corporate insider sentiment is negative due to increased selling.

feed.title: Bristol-Myers Squibb’s KarXT Safety Study: What Long-Term Urological Data Could Mean for BMY Investors
feed.summary: Bristol-Myers Squibb (BMY) is conducting a 12-month, phase 4 safety study for its KarXT drug, focusing on urological safety in schizophrenia patients. This study aims to assess long-term bladder function and urinary safety, which are critical factors for patient adherence and market adoption of psychiatric medications. Positive results could significantly de-risk KarXT and enhance its market potential by addressing common side-effect concerns associated with existing treatments.

feed.title: Amazon.com launches Health AI, its intelligent medical assistant for One Medical patients
feed.summary: Amazon has launched Health AI, an intelligent medical assistant for patients of its One Medical primary care network, acquired in 2023. This AI-powered tool, integrated into the One Medical app and leveraging Amazon Bedrock language models, provides personalized answers to health questions, helps manage treatments, and streamlines appointment scheduling. While it doesn't diagnose or prescribe, it directs patients to professionals when necessary and aims to enhance the patient journey by integrating technology while keeping human clinicians central to care.

feed.title: AWS Hires Microsoft Teams Founder To Lead Amazon Quick Suite
feed.summary: AWS has announced the hiring of Jigar Thakkar, a founding member and engineering leader of Microsoft Teams, to head its new Amazon Quick Suite. Thakkar, who spent 19 years at Microsoft and also served as CTO for MSCI Inc., will be responsible for developing a portfolio of agentic applications and services for business users within the AI-powered digital workspace. Quick Suite, which integrates Amazon Q Business and Amazon QuickSight, aims to help businesses derive insights, automate tasks, and is already used by Amazon employees and major clients like BMW and AstraZeneca.

feed.title: MGM Goes Green in Vegas with 100% Solar-Powered Daytime Electricity
feed.summary: MGM Resorts International announced a significant milestone in its renewable energy strategy, with its Las Vegas Strip casinos now being 100% powered by solar energy during the daytime. This achievement follows the integration of 115 MW of solar energy and 400 MWh of battery storage from the new Escape Solar and Storage Project, complementing its existing 100 MW Mega Solar Array. The company aims to use 100% renewable electricity domestically by 2030, demonstrating that sustainable practices are viable for large gaming enterprises.

feed.title: BVI appoints Jim Hollingshead as CEO
feed.summary: BVI, an ophthalmic device company, has appointed Jim Hollingshead, former CEO of Insulet, as its new President and CEO. Hollingshead will lead BVI's global eye care business, leveraging a recent $1 billion financing round to accelerate product innovation and expand its global footprint. He replaces Shervin Korangy, who will now serve as an adviser to BVI and senior adviser to TPG Capital, BVI's private equity owner.

feed.title: Palantir Is the Ideal AI Software for Governments and Enterprises but at a Large Price to Investors
feed.summary: Palantir is highlighted as a leading AI company that provides proprietary platforms to optimize decision-making for both governments and enterprises. Its systems aggregate, clean, and analyze complex datasets, delivering actionable insights and continuous improvements through machine-learning-based solutions. While the technology is praised for driving efficiency, the article notes its high valuation presents a significant cost to investors.

feed.title: How To YieldBoost Cboe Global Markets To 1.9% Using Options
feed.summary: This article explains how shareholders of Cboe Global Markets (CBOE) can potentially increase their annual return to 1.9% by selling a covered call option. By selling the January 2028 covered call at the $390 strike price, investors can collect a premium that adds 0.9% to their existing 1.1% dividend yield. This strategy involves the risk of losing upside above $390 if the stock is called away, though it would still result in a significant return from the current trading level.

feed.title: Assessing Dime Community Bancshares (DCOM) Valuation After Strong 2025 Earnings And Improved Credit Trends
feed.summary: Dime Community Bancshares (DCOM) has reported strong full-year 2025 earnings and improved credit trends, yet its stock has seen recent softening despite a strong five-year return. The company's P/E ratio of 26.3x screens as expensive compared to the US Banks industry average, implying high investor expectations despite a recent decline in net profit margins. However, a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model suggests the stock is undervalued by over 50%, trading significantly below an estimated fair value of US$60.90.

feed.title: YieldBoost EA From 0.4% To 3.4% Using Options
feed.summary: Electronic Arts (EA) shareholders can boost their income from 0.4% to an annualized 3.4% by selling February covered calls at the $205 strike price. This strategy involves collecting a premium and offers a significant return if the stock is not "called away," though upside beyond $205 would be foregone in that scenario. The article also provides historical dividend data and volatility analysis to help assess the risk-reward of this options strategy.

feed.title: D.R. Horton Stock Tests Support Following Earnings Report
feed.summary: D.R. Horton's stock is testing support levels after its Q1 2026 earnings report, which, despite beating estimates, showed year-over-year declines in revenue and EPS. The housing market continues to face pressure from elevated mortgage rates, requiring sales incentives to sustain demand, though tight inventory supports pricing power. Analysts have mixed sentiments, and the stock's future movement depends on whether it can maintain its 50-day simple moving average as support.

feed.title: Mars-Kellanova: The $36bn merger transforming the snacking sector
feed.summary: Mars' $36 billion acquisition of Kellanova has significantly reshaped the global snacking industry, combining major brands like Pringles, Cheez-It, Snickers, and M&M's under Mars Snacking. This merger strengthens Mars' market position, drives innovation in healthier and on-the-go snack options, and is expected to lead to increased M&A activity among competitors. Despite regulatory scrutiny and integration complexities, the deal positions Mars to influence consumer trends and market dynamics across the snacking sector.

feed.title: Power bills 28% lower: why Fortune just praised Xcel Energy
feed.summary: Xcel Energy has been recognized for the twelfth consecutive year as one of Fortune's "World's Most Admired Companies," ranking first in social responsibility and fourth overall among electric and gas utilities. The company was lauded for its innovation, financial soundness, and commitment to delivering affordable and reliable energy. Xcel Energy's dedication to strengthening its grid and achieving bill growth below the rate of inflation while keeping electric bills 28% below the national average were highlighted as key achievements.

feed.title: Bank of America drops bold call on Roblox, sees massive upside
feed.summary: Bank of America maintains a bullish stance on Roblox, predicting massive upside due to the platform's evolving ecosystem and capacity to consistently generate new popular experiences. Despite third-party data suggesting a decline in user hours for top games, BofA highlights a significant increase in engagement for less popular games, indicating a broader platform growth. The firm raised its Q4 forecast for Roblox's bookings and sees the stock as a "dip buying" opportunity, projecting a price of $171.

feed.title: Textron (NYSE:TXT) Hits New 12-Month High - Time to Buy?
feed.summary: Textron (NYSE:TXT) recently hit a new 52-week high, trading at $95.45 on Wednesday, driven by strong Q4 EPS results and positive analyst upgrades. The company exceeded earnings estimates with $1.55 EPS, and analysts have a "Moderate Buy" consensus with price targets ranging from $96.33 to $115. Textron has also provided optimistic FY2025 guidance, projecting EPS between $6.00 and $6.20.

feed.title: Clorox Q2 Preview: Private Label Pressure Clouds The Outlook (NYSE:CLX)
feed.summary: The Clorox Company (CLX) is expected to report weak fiscal Q2 results, influenced by shipment headwinds and slowing consumption. While the company projects growth reacceleration in H2, increasing private label penetration presents a significant threat. Despite these concerns, weak growth is already factored into the stock price, with an estimated fair value of $113.

feed.title: Southern Company ranked No. 1 in industry on FORTUNE's 2026 World's Most Admired Companies list
feed.summary: Southern Company has been recognized as the No. 1 company in the electric and gas utilities industry on FORTUNE's 2026 World's Most Admired Companies list. The company earned this distinction for its financial soundness, long-term investment value, and community commitment. Chairman, President, and CEO Chris Womack attributed this achievement to the dedication of its 28,000 employees, emphasizing their focus on strengthening service, meeting growing demand, and disciplined financial management.

feed.title: Raymond James expands western housing bench with Goldberg hire
feed.summary: Raymond James has hired Greg Goldberg, a veteran multifamily housing finance banker from RBC Capital Markets, to lead its western multifamily public finance coverage. Goldberg, based in Denver, will focus on expanding the firm's housing practice, particularly in affordable and workforce housing. This strategic hire aims to enhance Raymond James' national housing finance practice and broaden its geographical reach, reinforcing its position as a top municipal bond underwriter.

feed.title: Lululemon halts online sales of new collection after sheerness complaints
feed.summary: Lululemon has temporarily stopped online sales of its new "Get Low" activewear collection due to customer complaints about the fabric's sheerness and sizing issues. The company stated it is pausing sales to understand feedback and provide product education, with plans to restore online availability soon. This incident follows previous product problems for Lululemon, including a 2013 recall of sheer yoga pants.

feed.title: PR News | HSBC Alum Ward Joins FTI Consulting - Wed., Jan. 21, 2026
feed.summary: Matt Ward, formerly head of US communications at HSBC and Corebridge Financial, has joined FTI Consulting's PR operation unit in New York as a managing director. He will focus on providing communications services to banking clients, including executive positioning, crisis response, and digital reputation management. His experience spans both public and private sectors, enhancing FTI's financial services communications capabilities.
